back_translation: Abimelech son of Jerubbaal went to his mother's brothers at Shechem and said to them and to all the clan of his mother's father's house,

'Please ask all the leaders of Shechem, Which is better for you — that seventy men, all the sons of Jerubbaal, rule over you, or that one man rule over you? And remember that I am your bone and your flesh.'
His mother's brothers spoke all these words about him in the ears of all the leaders of Shechem, and their hearts inclined toward Abimelech, because they said, 'He is our brother.'
They gave him seventy shekels of silver from the temple of Baal-berith, with which Abimelech hired worthless and reckless men, and they followed him.
He went to his father's house in Ophrah and killed his brothers, the seventy sons of Jerubbaal, on one stone. But Jotham the youngest son of Jerubbaal survived, because he hid.
All the leaders of Shechem and all Beth-millo gathered and went and made Abimelech king at the oak of the pillar that was in Shechem.
When it was reported to Jotham, he went and stood on the top of Mount Gerizim, raised his voice, called out, and said to them, 'Listen to me, leaders of Shechem, that God may listen to you.'
'The trees once set out to anoint a king over themselves, and they said to the olive tree, Reign over us.'
'But the olive tree said to them, Should I cease giving my oil, by which God and men are honored, and go to sway over the trees?'
'Then the trees said to the fig tree, Come, you reign over us.'
'But the fig tree said to them, Should I cease giving my sweetness and my good fruit, and go to sway over the trees?'
'Then the trees said to the grapevine, Come, you reign over us.'
'But the grapevine said to them, Should I cease giving my wine, which cheers God and men, and go to sway over the trees?'
'Finally all the trees said to the thornbush, Come, you reign over us.'

'The thornbush said to the trees, If in truth you are anointing me as king over you, come and find refuge in my shade. But if not, may fire come out from the thornbush and consume the cedars of Lebanon.'
'Now if you have acted in truth and integrity in making Abimelech king, if you have done well by Jerubbaal and his house, and if you have done to him according to the deserving of his hands,'
'for my father fought for you and risked his life to deliver you from the hand of Midian —'

'but you have risen up against my father's house today and killed his seventy sons on one stone, and made Abimelech, son of his maidservant, king over the leaders of Shechem because he is your brother —'
'if you have acted in truth and integrity toward Jerubbaal and his house today, then rejoice in Abimelech, and let him also rejoice in you.'
'But if not, may fire come out from Abimelech and consume the leaders of Shechem and Beth-millo, and may fire come out from the leaders of Shechem and from Beth-millo and consume Abimelech!'
Then Jotham fled and ran away, and went to Beer, and dwelt there because of his brother Abimelech.
Abimelech ruled over Israel for three years.
God sent a spirit of enmity between Abimelech and the leaders of Shechem, and the leaders of Shechem dealt treacherously with Abimelech,

so that the violence done to the seventy sons of Jerubbaal might come back, and their blood might be placed on Abimelech their brother who had killed them, and on the leaders of Shechem who had strengthened his hands to kill his brothers.
The leaders of Shechem set ambushers against him on the hilltops, and they robbed everyone who passed by them on the road. It was reported to Abimelech.
Gaal son of Ebed came with his brothers and crossed over into Shechem, and the leaders of Shechem put their trust in him.
They went out into the fields, gathered grapes from their vineyards, trod them, made a festival, went into the temple of their god, ate and drank, and cursed Abimelech.

Gaal son of Ebed said, 'Who is Abimelech, and who is Shechem, that we should serve him? Is he not the son of Jerubbaal, and is not Zebul his officer? Serve the men of Hamor the father of Shechem! Why should we serve him?'
'If only this people were under my hand, I would remove Abimelech, and I would say to him, Muster your army and come out!'
When Zebul the officer of the city heard the words of Gaal son of Ebed, his anger burned.
He sent messengers covertly to Abimelech, saying, 'Behold, Gaal son of Ebed and his brothers are coming to Shechem, and behold, they are stirring up the city against you.'
'Now then, arise by night, you and the people with you, and lie in wait in the fields.'
'In the morning, as the sun rises, you shall get up early and rush against the city. When he and the people with him come out against you, you may do to him as your hand finds.'
So Abimelech and all the people with him arose by night, and they lay in wait against Shechem in four companies.
Gaal son of Ebed went out and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city, and Abimelech and the people with him arose from the ambush.
When Gaal saw the people, he said to Zebul, 'Look, people are coming down from the tops of the mountains!' Zebul said to him, 'You are seeing the shadow of the mountains as people.'
Gaal spoke again and said, 'Look, people are coming down from the center of the land, and one company is coming by the way of the oak of the soothsayers.'
Zebul said to him, 'Where is your mouth now, with which you said, Who is Abimelech that we should serve him? Are these not the people you despised? Go out now and fight against them!'
Gaal went out before the leaders of Shechem and fought against Abimelech.
Abimelech pursued him, and he fled before him. Many wounded fell, all the way to the entrance of the gate.
Abimelech stayed at Arumah, and Zebul drove Gaal and his brothers out from dwelling in Shechem.
On the next day the people went out into the fields, and they reported it to Abimelech.
He took his men and divided them into three companies, and they lay in wait in the fields. He looked, and behold, the people were coming out of the city, and he rose up against them and struck them.
Abimelech and the companies with him rushed forward and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city, while two companies rushed against all who were in the fields and struck them.
Abimelech fought against the city all that day, captured the city, and killed the people who were in it. He demolished the city and sowed it with salt.
When all the leaders of the tower of Shechem heard this, they entered the inner chamber of the temple of El-berith.
It was reported to Abimelech that all the leaders of the tower of Shechem had gathered there.

Abimelech went up Mount Zalmon, he and all the people with him. Abimelech took an axe in his hand, cut a branch from the trees, lifted it, and placed it on his shoulder. He said to the people with him, 'What you have seen me do, hurry and do as I have done.'

Each man also cut his own branch and followed Abimelech. They piled them on the inner chamber and set the chamber on fire over them. So all the people of the tower of Shechem also died, about a thousand men and women.
Abimelech went to Thebez, encamped against Thebez, and captured it.
There was a strong tower in the middle of the city, and all the men and women and all the leaders of the city fled there. They locked themselves in and went up on the roof of the tower.
Abimelech came to the tower and fought against it, approaching the entrance of the tower to burn it with fire.
But a certain woman threw an upper millstone onto Abimelech's head and crushed his skull.
He quickly called to the young man who carried his weapons and said to him, 'Draw your sword and kill me, lest they say of me, A woman killed him.' So his attendant pierced him through, and he died.
When the men of Israel saw that Abimelech was dead, each went to his place.
Thus God repaid the wickedness of Abimelech that he did to his father in killing his seventy brothers.
And all the wickedness of the men of Shechem God brought back upon their heads, and the curse of Jotham son of Jerubbaal came upon them.
